questions.question: What is the normal adult blood pressure?
questions.explanation: Normal adult blood pressure is approximately 120/80 mmHg.
questions.source: INC GNM Syllabus / Standard Nursing Textbooks
questions.verified_by: GNM syllabus cross-check + INC textbook review

questions.question: How many chambers are present in the human heart?
questions.explanation: The human heart has four chambers: two atria and two ventricles.
questions.source: INC GNM Syllabus / Standard Nursing Textbooks
questions.verified_by: GNM syllabus cross-check + INC textbook review

questions.question: What is the normal respiratory rate for a healthy adult?
questions.explanation: The normal adult respiratory rate is 12-20 breaths per minute.
questions.source: INC GNM Syllabus / Standard Nursing Textbooks
questions.verified_by: GNM syllabus cross-check + INC textbook review

questions.question: Which enzyme is primarily responsible for protein digestion in the stomach?
questions.explanation: Pepsin is secreted by the chief cells of the stomach and begins protein digestion.
questions.source: INC GNM Syllabus / Standard Nursing Textbooks
questions.verified_by: GNM syllabus cross-check + INC textbook review

questions.question: What is the functional unit of the kidney?
questions.explanation: The nephron is the functional unit of the kidney where urine is formed.
questions.source: INC GNM Syllabus / Standard Nursing Textbooks
questions.verified_by: GNM syllabus cross-check + INC textbook review
